project.name: Cutline
project.description: Script to video platform for filmmakers

paradigm.name: Shot-List-First
paradigm.description: The confirmed shot list is the specification for storyboard generation
rules: AI generates storyboards from confirmed shots only

architecture.type: Full-stack web application with PWA functionality

frontend.pwa: Service worker for offline support, installable on desktop and mobile

features: API key proxy for AI services

rationale.fullStackMVP: Complete web application from the start - provides authentication, persistence, and API security while maintaining PWA offline capabilities
rationale.bunElysiaSQLite: Bun fast all-in-one runtime with native TypeScript, Elysia type-safe with excellent DX, SQLite lightweight and embedded - perfect for single-server deployment
rationale.pwaFunctionality: Progressive Web App capabilities enable offline use and app-like experience while backend handles sync, auth, and secure API key management

1.name: Script Input & Parsing
1.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to upload or paste a script in Fountain format so that the AI can automatically recognize and organize its elements.
acceptanceCriteria: Accept Fountain format script input (paste or file upload)
Parse and identify scenes (scene headings, sluglines)
Parse and identify characters
Parse and identify dialogue
Parse and identify action lines
Parse and identify transitions
Parse and identify parentheticals
Display parsed structure in organized, navigable format
Handle common Fountain format variations

1.5.name: Script Editor
1.5.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to write and edit scripts directly in the application so that I can work on my screenplay without switching tools.
acceptanceCriteria: In-app Fountain format script editor with syntax highlighting
Auto-formatting as you type (sluglines, character names, dialogue)
Fountain format autocomplete
Scene heading detection and formatting
Character name caching and autocomplete
Standard screenplay formatting (Courier font, proper margins/spacing)
AV Format Support: Two-column script format (Video | Audio columns)
Keyboard shortcuts for common script elements (Tab for character, Enter for dialogue, etc.)
Word count and page count estimation
Save indicator for unsaved changes
Responsive design: editing works on desktop, tablet, and mobile

1.6.name: Script Breakdown & Analysis
1.6.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to see my script automatically broken down into its constituent elements so that I can understand and organize the production requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract scenes: All scene headings with line numbers, INT/EXT classification
Extract characters: All speaking characters with dialogue counts and scene appearances
Extract locations: All unique locations grouped by interior/exterior
Extract props: Key props mentioned in action lines (AI-detected)
Extract time of day: Day/night/dawn/dusk classification per scene
Extract transitions: All transitions between scenes
Visual breakdown dashboard with scene list, character roster, location list
Script statistics (total scenes, characters, locations, estimated duration)
Click-through navigation: click any element to jump to its location in script
Export breakdown as CSV or PDF
Scene metadata editing: add notes, tags, and production status
Integration with Shot List: Characters and locations available as dropdowns

1.7.name: Shot List Editor
1.7.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to define shots for each scene so that the AI knows exactly what storyboards to generate.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-scene shot list editor with tabular interface
Shot Number: Sequential (1, 2, 3...)
Shot Type: Wide / Medium / Close-up / Extreme CU / Two-shot / Over-the-shoulder / Establishing / Insert
Camera Angle: Eye level / High angle / Low angle / Dutch angle / Bird's eye / Worm's eye
Camera Movement: Static / Pan / Tilt / Dolly / Truck / Pedestal / Arc / Handheld / Steadicam
Characters in Frame: Select from script's character roster
Action Description: Brief description of what happens in shot
Duration: Suggested length (seconds)
Notes: Director's additional notes
Keyboard shortcuts: Tab to next field, Enter to add new shot, Ctrl+Z undo
Copy/duplicate shot functionality
Reorder shots (drag and drop or move up/down buttons)
Delete shot with confirmation
Shot count display per scene
Auto-save shot list changes

1.8.name: AI-Assisted Shot Suggestions
1.8.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want the AI to suggest appropriate shots for my scenes so that I have a starting point rather than starting from blank.
acceptanceCriteria: AI analyzes scene content and suggests initial shot list
Dialogue scenes: Suggest establishing wide, two-shot medium, close-ups on speaking characters
Action scenes: Suggest dynamic coverage with multiple angles
Montage sequences: Suggest quick-cut shots with variety
Reveal moments: Suggest specific close-up or insert shots
AI provides reasoning for each suggestion
Director can accept all suggestions
Director can accept individual shots
Director can edit any suggested shot
Director can delete suggestions and add own shots
Regenerate suggestions button with different prompt options
Suggestions use scene context (characters, location, mood from breakdown)

1.9.name: Shot List Confirmation
1.9.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to explicitly confirm my shot list before AI generation so that I don't waste credits on unwanted storyboards.
acceptanceCriteria: Review mode showing all shots in the scene
Summary statistics: shot count, estimated storyboard generation cost
Confirm Shot List button that locks the shot list
Confirm enables AI storyboard generation for this scene
Confirm shows confirmation with estimated cost
Unlock for Editing button to make changes after confirmation
Visual indicator showing confirmed vs. unconfirmed scenes
Bulk confirmation: confirm multiple scenes at once

1.10.name: Local Collaboration Features
1.10.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to add notes and track changes without cloud sync so that I can iterate on my script locally.
acceptanceCriteria: Add notes to scenes, storyboards, characters, and shots
Inline comments on script text
Per-scene notes panel
Per-shot notes in shot list
Save Version with name and description
View version list with timestamps
Restore any previous version
Compare versions (see what changed)
Toggle track changes mode
See who changed what (local user only for Phase 1)
Accept or reject individual changes
All collaboration data stored locally (IndexedDB)

1.11.name: Progressive Web App Features
1.11.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to install Cutline as an app and work offline so that I can write and plan anywhere, with automatic sync when connected.
acceptanceCriteria: Installable PWA (Add to Home Screen on mobile/desktop)
Offline mode: core editing features available without internet
Background sync when connection restored to backend
Desktop editing (full keyboard shortcuts, multi-panel)
Tablet editing (touch-optimized, responsive panels)
Mobile editing (simplified interface, still fully functional)
Local data persistence (IndexedDB) as offline cache
Automatic sync with backend when online
Conflict resolution for offline edits

2.name: Script-Level Configuration
2.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to define overall visual style and settings at the script level so that the AI generates storyboards that match my creative vision from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: Configure overall visual style (cinematic, documentary, animation, etc.)
Configure color palette preferences
Configure tone/mood (dark, lighthearted, suspenseful, etc.)
Create character profiles with text descriptions
Include: age, physical traits, clothing style, typical expressions, personality notes
AI generates visual reference images from character descriptions
Director approves or refines generated reference images
Approved references used for consistent character rendering across all storyboards
Script-level settings inform AI's initial storyboard generation

3.name: Scene-Level Configuration
3.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to define scene-specific visual style and setting details so that all storyboards within that scene maintain consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: Configure location/set description (environment details)
Configure mood/atmosphere (tense, warm, eerie, etc.)
Configure lighting style (harsh, soft, golden hour, etc.)
Configure color palette adjustments (override script defaults if needed)
Configure overall visual style/tone for this scene
Scene-level settings apply as defaults to all storyboards in that scene
Ability to override scene defaults at individual storyboard level

4.name: Storyboard-Level Configuration
4.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to control specific shot details for each storyboard panel so that I can direct the visual narrative precisely.
acceptanceCriteria: Configure shot composition (camera angles, framing, movement)
Configure character appearance and positioning
Configure character expressions and emotions
Configure specific camera movements within the shot
Inherit scene-level defaults by default
Override scene defaults when needed
Preview/visualize storyboard panel configuration

5.name: Image Generation API Integration
5.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to choose between multiple image generation APIs so that I can optimize for cost, quality, and regional availability.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary API - SDXL: Lowest cost (~$0.002/image)
Primary API - SDXL: Good quality for storyboard aesthetics
Primary API - SDXL: Best for international users
Primary API - SDXL: Multiple style LoRAs available
Secondary API - Wan2.6: Excellent for China-based users
Secondary API - Wan2.6: Superior text rendering and layout control
Secondary API - Wan2.6: Native sketch/line art control via ControlNet
Secondary API - Wan2.6: Pricing ~¥0.20/image (~$0.028)
User can choose default API at project level
Per-scene API override option
Auto-select based on user region (China → Wan2.6, International → SDXL)
Both APIs support sketch/illustrated/manga aesthetics
Style prompts adapted per API
Character reference system works with both APIs
Automatic retry with alternative API if primary fails
Rate limit handling and queuing
Clear error messages if API unavailable
Show estimated cost before generating storyboards
Track API usage and costs per project
Budget warnings (optional threshold)
implementationNotes: Use Alibaba Cloud DashScope API for Wan2.6
Abstraction layer for easy addition of future APIs

6.name: AI Storyboard Generation
6.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want the AI to generate storyboard panels based on my confirmed shot list so that I get visual representations of my planned shots.
acceptanceCriteria: REQUIRES confirmed shot list (Feature #1.9)
For each confirmed shot, generates ONE storyboard panel
Shot Type influences framing and composition
Camera Angle influences perspective in generated image
Camera Movement hints at motion within static image
Characters in Frame ensures correct characters appear
Action Description is main prompt content
Visual Style: Static AI images with sketch/illustrated/manga aesthetic
Style options: Pencil sketch, Ink drawing, Manga/comic, Watercolor storyboard
Style selection at script or scene level
Consistent style within a scene
Progress indicator: Generating storyboard X of Y for Scene Z
Cost tracking: show API cost per panel and running total
Batch generation: generate all storyboards for a scene at once
Individual regeneration: regenerate specific panels after viewing
Fast generation (~2-5 seconds per panel)

7.name: Storyboard Refinement
7.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to selectively edit individual storyboard panels with targeted prompts so that I can refine the AI's rough drafts without starting from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: Each storyboard panel has an Edit button
Edit mode opens prompt input for that specific panel
Director enters refinement prompt
AI regenerates that single panel based on prompt
Previous version saved (undo/redo capability)
Non-destructive editing (can always revert to rough draft)

8.name: Video Generation - Scene Level
8.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to generate video clips for all storyboard panels in a scene at once so that I can efficiently create the visual content for that scene.
acceptanceCriteria: Select a scene and click Generate Video
Generates individual video clips for each storyboard panel in that scene
Batch processing for efficiency
Progress indicator showing which panels are complete
Generated clips are organized by scene and panel
Video generation parameters can be set at scene level
Duration per clip configurable
Motion intensity (subtle to dynamic) configurable
Resolution/quality settings configurable
Store generation metadata (API used, parameters, timestamp) for reproducibility

9.name: Video Generation - Panel Preview
9.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to generate a single storyboard panel into video so that I can preview how it will look before committing to generating the full scene.
acceptanceCriteria: Preview Video button on individual storyboard panels
Generates video for that single panel only
Uses same scene-level generation parameters
Faster feedback for iterative refinement
Can regenerate panel with different prompts/settings

10.name: Video Export & Assembly
10.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to export my generated video clips so that I can use them in post-production or share them.
acceptanceCriteria: Export individual video clips per storyboard panel (primary)
Download as organized folder structure
Include metadata file with generation details
Future phase: Assemble full video with transitions
Export options: resolution, frame rate, format (MP4, etc.)

11.name: Video Generation API Integration
11.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to select from multiple video generation APIs so that I can choose the best option for my needs (cost, quality, speed).
acceptanceCriteria: Support RunwayML (Gen-2/Gen-3)
Support Pika Labs
Support Stability AI
Easy to add new integrations
API selection at project or scene level
Display API status (available, rate limits, costs if known)
Prototype phase: Implement with easiest/cheapest option first
Fallback/error handling if API is unavailable

12.name: Project Saving & Auto-Save
12.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want my work to be automatically saved locally so that I don't lose progress if I accidentally close the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Auto-save to local storage (browser IndexedDB)
Save all project data: script, breakdown, shot lists, configurations, storyboards, generated assets, comments, versions
Auto-save every 30 seconds or on significant changes
Manual Save button for immediate save
Save As to create new project file
Visual indicator showing unsaved changes

13.name: Project Export/Import
13.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to export my project as a file so that I can back it up, share it, or work on it across different devices.
acceptanceCriteria: Export project as JSON file
Include all project data and metadata
Import project files to restore or continue work
Validate imported files for compatibility
Handle import conflicts (version mismatch, missing data)

14.name: Project Versioning
14.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to save multiple versions of my project so that I can experiment with different creative directions and revert if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Create named versions (V1 - Original, V2 - Darker mood)
Save snapshot of entire project state
View version history with timestamps and notes
Restore any previous version
Compare versions (see what changed between versions)
Branch/merge functionality (optional, advanced)

15.name: Project Duplication
15.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to duplicate my project so that I can create variations without losing the original.
acceptanceCriteria: Duplicate Project button
Creates complete copy with new name
All configurations, storyboards, and generated assets copied
Prompt for new project name
Keep reference to original project

16.name: Fountain Script Import with Smart Configuration
16.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to import an existing Fountain script and have the system intelligently suggest configurations so that I don't have to start from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: Upload Fountain script (.fountain or .txt)
Parse script and identify scenes, characters, locations
AI analyzes script and suggests script-level visual style based on genre/tone
AI suggests character profiles from dialogue and action descriptions
AI suggests scene mood/lighting based on scene descriptions
Director reviews and accepts/edits suggestions
Generate initial rough draft storyboards automatically

17.name: Backend API & Authentication
17.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to create an account and have my projects securely stored and synced so that I can access them from any device.
acceptanceCriteria: User signup with email and password
User login with JWT token authentication
Secure password hashing (bcrypt)
Project persistence to SQLite database
API key proxy for AI services (keys never exposed to client)
RESTful API with type-safe routes
CORS configuration for frontend
Error handling with appropriate HTTP status codes

17.5.name: Offline Sync & Collaboration
17.5.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want my offline edits to automatically sync when I'm back online so that I never lose work.
acceptanceCriteria: Automatic sync when connection restored
Conflict resolution for concurrent edits
Real-time sync across devices
Share projects with specific users
Permission levels (view, edit, admin)
Comments/annotations on storyboards
Project history and activity log

18.name: Scene-Focused Main View
18.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to focus on one scene at a time so that I can work through my script methodically without being overwhelmed.
acceptanceCriteria: Main view displays current scene with its storyboard panels
Left Sidebar: Project name and settings
Left Sidebar: Script overview (scene list with scene headings)
Left Sidebar: Character list (quick access to character profiles)
Left Sidebar: Scene navigation (previous/next, jump to scene)
Left Sidebar: Global settings (script-level config)
Main Content: Current scene heading (slugline)
Main Content: Scene-level settings panel (collapsible)
Main Content: Storyboard panels displayed in sequence
Main Content: Timeline/filmstrip view of panels
Main Content: Script text for this scene
Right Panel: Contextual tools (edit prompts, settings, video generation)
Top Navigation: Breadcrumb navigation
Top Navigation: View mode toggles (Storyboard / Script / Split)
Top Navigation: Export / Save / Share buttons

19.name: Storyboard Panel Interaction
19.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to interact with individual storyboard panels naturally so that I can review and refine them efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Storyboard panels displayed in horizontal scrollable strip (filmstrip style)
Click panel to select/focus it
Selected panel shows in larger detail view
Panel Actions: Edit button (opens edit prompt panel)
Panel Actions: Regenerate button (create new version)
Panel Actions: Duplicate button (copy panel)
Panel Actions: Delete button (remove panel)
Panel Actions: Move/reorder buttons (shift left/right)
Panel Detail View: Large preview of storyboard image
Panel Detail View: Associated script text (action lines, dialogue)
Panel Detail View: Panel metadata (shot type, characters in frame, location)
Panel Detail View: Version history (if edited multiple times)
Keyboard navigation: Arrow keys to move between panels

20.name: Script View Integration
20.userStory: As a filmmaker, I want to see the original script text alongside storyboards so that I can ensure the visual interpretation matches my written words.
acceptanceCriteria: Script View Mode: Full parsed script display
Script View Mode: Fountain format highlighting
Script View Mode: Click scene heading to jump to that scene
Script View Mode: Click character name to view character profile
Script View Mode: Editable script text (Fountain format)
Split View Mode: Script on left, storyboards on right
Split View Mode: Synced scrolling
Split View Mode: Click action line → highlight corresponding storyboard panel
Split View Mode: Click storyboard panel → highlight script text
Storyboard View Mode: Hide script, focus on visuals (default)

criteria.evidence: app/client/package.json exists with vite, react, typescript dependencies
package.json includes react-query, zustand, dexie, fp-ts, react-router-dom

criteria.item: TypeScript strict mode enabled with path aliases

criteria.item: Vite configured with PWA plugin and code splitting
criteria.evidence: vite.config.ts has VitePWA plugin and manualChunks configuration
lib/fp/ has option.ts, result.ts, async-result.ts, errors.ts with 132/133 tests passing
types/index.ts has Shot, Scene, Project, StoryboardPanel with confirmation state
test-utils/index.ts has createTestDB, createTestShot, assertion helpers
App.tsx has routes for project workspace, script editor, shot list, storyboards

criteria.item: Dexie database schema with all tables
criteria.evidence: db.ts has all 8 tables: projects, scripts, scenes, shots, storyboards, characters, comments, versions

criteria.item: Shots indexed by sceneId and confirmed state

criteria.evidence: All 8 repositories implemented: ProjectRepository, SceneRepository, ScriptRepository, ShotRepository, CharacterRepository, CommentRepository, StoryboardRepository, VersionRepository
7 adapters implemented: project-adapter, scene-adapter, script-adapter, shot-adapter, character-adapter, comment-adapter, storyboard-adapter, version-adapter

criteria.item: Zustand UI store with selection and view state
criteria.evidence: ui-store.ts with view mode, current project/scene/shot IDs

criteria.item: TanStack Query integration for data fetching
criteria.evidence: use-projects, use-scenes, use-shots, use-storyboards hooks implemented

criteria.item: Fountain parser parses INT/EXT scene headings
criteria.evidence: fountain-parser.test.ts: All scene heading tests pass - INT, EXT, INT/EXT correctly parsed

criteria.item: Fountain parser extracts character names and dialogue
criteria.evidence: fountain-parser.test.ts: Character dialogue extraction and counting tests pass
ScriptEditor component with FountainHighlight for real-time syntax highlighting

criteria.item: Shot type validation (wide, medium, close-up, etc.)
criteria.evidence: ShotTypeSelect, CameraAngleSelect, CameraMovementSelect components with validation

criteria.item: Shots created in unconfirmed state by default
confirmShotList sets confirmed=true and confirmedAt on all shots
Exactly ONE panel generated per confirmed shot

criteria.evidence: AIProxyService.buildShotPrompt extracts shot type, angle, movement, action
sdxlGenerate() calls Replicate API with polling for results
useGenerateStoryboards hook provides onProgress callback with GenerationStatus
auth routes with bcrypt password hashing

criteria.item: Shot CRUD routes with paradigm gates
API key proxy for AI services
criteria.evidence: AI proxy service exists, needs provider wiring

testCoverage.note: Estimated based on repository/adapter/service test coverage

knownIssues.description: Timing assertion too strict - expects <100ms but takes ~238ms
knownIssues.suggestedFix: Increase threshold to 300ms or use fake timers

priority.item: E2E integration tests for full workflow
Minor UI polish and error handling
API key management UI for users
